verdict_detail: Cross-block h1 closes the bonding-orbital-construction mechanism of the W1c-residual wall (natural occupations transform from [1, 1] to [1.9991, 0.0007], dominant NO bonding with 50/50 Na/H split, lowest h1 eigenvector is now bonding) but does NOT close the inner-region overattraction mechanism (PES still monotonically descending with R_min at 2.0 bohr, D_e well depth +4.37 Ha vs experimental 0.075 Ha).

step1_diagnostic.note: Both bases pass the >50 mHa bonding threshold by several orders of magnitude. PROCEED to Step 2 confirmed.

step2_production_wiring.backward_compat: bit-exact preserved across NaH/LiH/W1c/mz baseline regression
step2_production_wiring.regression: 146 passed, 1 skipped (128 baseline + 18 new), zero regression

F1_maxn3_W1c_mz_alone.naturals_at_R3.5: bonding orbital constructed by NOs (1.95, 0.05) but D_e^PES = +0.71 Ha spurious

comparison_to_baselines.F2_diagnosis: Architectural absence: cross-block h1 slot does not exist in composed_qubit
comparison_to_baselines.F3_advance: Bonding-orbital construction at h1 level IS the missing piece. Cross-block h1 transforms naturals from [1,1] to [1.999, 0.001] (4 orders of magnitude advance) and h1 lowest eigvec is now bonding (was antibonding under W1c+mz alone).

wall_classification_update.before_F3: W1d (architectural absence of cross-block h1 slot)
wall_classification_update.after_F3: W1d-closed (cross-block h1 added) BUT new sub-layer surfaced:
wall_classification_update.new_sub_layer: W1e: inner-region overattraction (missing Pauli repulsion mechanism between 2-electron bonding pair and frozen-core orbitals on Na). The 2-electron FCI on NaH max_n=2 has no explicit frozen-core electrons; W1c provides screening at the cross-V_ne level only, not full PK-projection. Cross-block h1 lowers the bonding-orbital energy across all R (since the matrix elements grow as orbitals overlap at smaller R), with no opposing repulsion. Inner-region collapse is the result.

interpretation: Cross-block h1 OVER-corrects: it injects bonding mechanism but does not include the opposing Pauli repulsion that would form an internal minimum. The structural picture sharpens: cross-block h1 is necessary but not sufficient.
